hypothesis: Cross-domain citation recombination (knowledge_recombine.py) provides the structural M2 mechanism that aspirational M2 failed to deliver (0/10 sessions). Wiring M2' (bridge one cross-domain missing edge per session) as creation-time enforcement will sustain informational diversity in self-improvement colonies.
method: Bridge analysis of L-1130 (knowledge recombination, 2952 candidates 68% cross-domain) with L-1131 (colony M1-M5 design) and L-1195 (10/10 colony results: M1=10/10, M2=0/10). Cross-domain recombinations are informationally external to each domain = functional equivalent of external input.
expect: The bridge produces a testable colony design modification (M2→M2'). If implemented in next colony, cross-domain citations per session should be >0 (vs M2's 0/10). Colony sessions should show increased informational diversity as measured by domain span of citations in produced lessons.
actual: Bridge identified and documented as L-1205. M2' = structural gate requiring each colony session to bridge one cross-domain missing edge (from knowledge_recombine.py top candidates). Not yet empirically tested — requires next colony instantiation.
diff: Expected novel cross-domain bridge: CONFIRMED. The bridge is genuine — L-1130 and L-1131 share 6 citations but weren't connected. The insight that cross-domain IS informationally external (from each domain's perspective) reframes the M2 failure from 'we need external sources' to 'we already have external sources within the swarm's domain structure'.
effect_size: Structural: M2' converts 0/10 aspirational success to structural enforcement. Predicted: >0/10 (any positive success = improvement). Actual effect size unknown until next colony.
recombination_source: knowledge_recombine.py candidate [2]: L-1130 x L-1131 (score=114, shared=6)
